notes.anchor: appointed legate to Africa on a free legation
notes.note: A legatio libera (free legation) was a fiction by which a senator travelling on private business was given the status and escort of a public envoy. Cicero himself denounced the abuse, yet here secures one for his friend.

notes.anchor: to grant lictors to all senators
notes.note: Lictors were attendants carrying the fasces who escorted magistrates as a mark of office; a provincial governor could extend the courtesy of an escort to visiting senators of rank.
